name: Gambia (Republic of The Gambia)
summary: The Gambia is a West African mixed-legal-system republic combining English common-law substantive heritage (via colonial inheritance) with Islamic-law personal-status jurisdiction (operating through Cadi/Sharia Courts under the Cadi Court Act) and customary-law jurisdiction via District Tribunals. Family-law framework operates under the Children's Act 2005, the Christian Marriage Act, the Mohammedan Marriage and Divorce Act, the Civil Marriage Act, and the Customary Marriage and Divorce Act. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Children's Act Part IV. The Supreme Court of The Gambia is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the High Court (Family Division), Magistrates' Courts, Cadi Courts for Muslim personal-status matters, and District Tribunals for customary-law matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. The Gambia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the best-interests-of-the-child principle codified in Children's Act s. 4. The Gambia is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Children's Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children's Act codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 4), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions.

statutory_framework.title: Cadi Court Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing Cadi Courts for Muslim personal-status matters.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of The Gambia
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, The Gambia
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Gambian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Gambia independence + Dawda Jawara founding-prime-minister + Cadi Court Act
key_developments.description: The Gambia achieved independence 18 February 1965 from the United Kingdom — establishing constitutional-monarchy framework. Dawda Jawara first prime minister 1965-1994 substantively significant founding-prime-minister framework. Cadi Court Act enacted establishing Sharia courts for Muslim personal-status matters (Muslim-majority ~95% population).

key_developments.title: Republic transition + Dawda Jawara presidency framework
key_developments.description: The Gambia became a republic 24 April 1970 — Dawda Jawara presidency 1970-1994 substantively significant African long-presidency framework (29 years total). Foundational pre-1994-coup framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 1981 Gambia coup attempt + Senegambia Confederation
key_developments.description: 1981 Gambia coup attempt 30 July 1981 led by Kukoi Samba Sanyang substantively disrupting political-institutional framework — Senegalese military intervention restored Jawara. Subsequent Senegambia Confederation 1982-1989 substantively distinctive West African confederation framework. Substantive bi-national-confederation framework affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: Gambia ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: The Gambia ratified the UNCRC on 8 August 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent Children's Act 2005 substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Yahya Jammeh coup d'état + 22-year-authoritarian framework
key_developments.description: Yahya Jammeh coup d'état 22 July 1994 ending Jawara presidency — substantively distinctive African 22-year-authoritarian framework 1994-2017. Substantive political-institutional disruption affecting family-law-implementation framework persisting through subsequent democratic-transition trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution of the Gambia 1997 + Jammeh-era constitutional framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of the Second Republic of The Gambia 1997 adopted 8 August 1996 (effective 16 January 1997) establishing Jammeh-era presidential republic framework. Foundational pre-2017-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Children's Act 2005 + child-protection-framework consolidation
key_developments.description: Federal Children's Act enacted codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 4), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions. Substantively significant statutory-modernisation within West African mixed-legal-system framework.

key_developments.title: Women's (Amendment) Act 2015 + FGM Prohibition framework + child-protection-expansion
key_developments.description: Women's (Amendment) Act 2015 enacted prohibiting female genital mutilation (FGM) — substantively significant child-protection-framework expansion. Substantive within-mixed-legal-system framework affecting customary-law and Islamic-law personal-status frameworks.

key_developments.title: Adama Barrow + Jammeh exile + democratic-transition framework
key_developments.description: Adama Barrow elected President 1 December 2016 ending 22-year Jammeh era. Jammeh exile January 2017 substantively distinctive African post-authoritarian democratic-transition framework. Substantive Truth, Reconciliation and Reparations Commission (TRRC) 2018-2021 framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: NHRC + FGM-repeal debate 2024 + Barrow continuing-presidency framework
key_developments.description: National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Children's Act 2005 + Cadi Court Act + Customary Marriage and Divorce Act framework. FGM-repeal debate 2023-2024 substantively significant institutional context — Bill seeking to repeal Women's (Amendment) Act 2015 FGM prohibition rejected by National Assembly 15 July 2024. Adama Barrow continuing-presidency framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant West African institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of The Gambia + Cadi Courts + District Tribunals — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + post-transition framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of The Gambia and Cadi Courts (for Muslim personal-status matters) and District Tribunals (for customary-law) continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Children's Act 2005 s. 4 + Cadi Court Act + Customary Marriage and Divorce Act framework in custody disputes within post-Jammeh transition framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: The Gambia operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — English common-law substantive heritage + Christian/Mohammedan/Civil/Customary marriage frameworks + Cadi Courts for Muslim personal-status + District Tribunals for customary-law. Within the West African common-law cluster with Nigeria, Ghana, Sierra Leone.
Cadi Court Act 1965 dedicated Sharia court framework is structurally distinctive within West Africa within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places The Gambia in the non-Hague West African cluster.
editorial_notes: The Gambia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Jawara-to-Barrow trajectory: 1965-Gambia-independence-+-Dawda-Jawara-+-Cadi-Court-Act + 1970-Republic-transition-+-Jawara-presidency + 1981-1981-Gambia-coup-attempt-+-Senegambia-Confederation + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1994-Yahya-Jammeh-coup-+-22-year-authoritarian + 1997-Constitution-of-the-Gambia-+-Jammeh-era + 2005-Children's-Act + 2015-Women's-Amendment-Act-FGM-Prohibition + 2017-Adama-Barrow-+-Jammeh-exile-+-democratic-transition + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Cadi-Courts-+-District-Tribunals-best-interests.

Mixed-legal-system West Africa (English common-law substantive + multi-track marriage frameworks + Cadi Courts for Muslim personal-status + District Tribunals for customary-law). Children's Act 2005 + Cadi Court Act 1965 + non-Hague Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Children's Act 2005 s. 4 + Cadi Court Act + customary-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins West-African + common-law + multi-track-Christian-Mohammedan-Civil-Customary-marriage + Cadi-Sharia-courts + Senegambia-Confederation-1982-1989-bi-national-distinctive + 1981-Gambia-coup-attempt-Senegalese-intervention + Yahya-Jammeh-22-year-authoritarian-1994-2017 + Adama-Barrow-democratic-transition-2017-+-Jammeh-exile + TRRC-2018-2021-distinctive-post-authoritarian-Truth-Reconciliation + Women's-Amendment-Act-2015-FGM-Prohibition + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
